# UC-19.2.37 · Dell VxRail vSAN Cluster Free Capacity and Rebuild Headroom

## Description

VxRail vSAN needs slack space to absorb rebuilds after a host or disk failure. Monitoring logical free percentage and vSAN slack metrics together explains when the cluster can no longer tolerate the policy’s `Failures to tolerate` during maintenance.

## Value

Supports safe one-node maintenance by proving enough free slack exists for resync before taking a host down.

## Implementation

Schedule a job that captures vSAN cluster capacity summaries per VxRail cluster ID. Map VMware fields such as `freeCapacity` and `totalCapacity` into TB. Join with policy summary if available. Tune thresholds to your FTT=1 vs FTT=2 designs.

## SPL

```spl
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vsan_capacity" earliest=-6h
| eval free_pct=100*((total_capacity_tb-used_capacity_tb)/total_capacity_tb)
| eval slack_pct=if(isnotnull(slack_space_tb), 100*(slack_space_tb/total_capacity_tb), null())
| where free_pct<=25 OR slack_pct<=5
| stats latest(free_pct) as free_pct, latest(slack_pct) as slack_pct, latest(host_failures_to_tolerate) as hftt by cluster_id
| sort free_pct
```

## Visualization

Cluster treemap (used vs free), Single value (clusters under runway threshold), Timechart (free_pct).

## References

- [VMware vSAN Design Guide — sizing slack space](https://core.vmware.com/resource/vmware-vsan-design-guide)
